WorkshopSubmission.title: WorkshopSubmission
WorkshopSubmission.description: Represents a workshop submission proposal from a partner.

id.description: Unique identifier for the workshop submission.

userId.description: Reference to User who submitted the workshop. (Relationship: User 1:N WorkshopSubmission)

alpfaPillar.description: The ALPFA pillar that the workshop aligns with.

sessionFormat.description: The format of the workshop session (e.g., lecture, panel, interactive).

intendedAudience.description: The intended audience for the workshop.

sessionTitle.description: The title of the workshop session.

sessionDescription.description: A detailed description of the workshop session.

objectives.description: The learning objectives for the workshop participants.

cpeCreditOption.description: Indicates whether the workshop offers CPE credits.

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the workshop submission was created.

status.description: The current status of the submission (e.g., 'waiting for approval', 'approved', 'rejected').

feedbackQueueId.description: Reference to the AI Feedback queue related to this submission. (Relationship: FeedbackQueue 1:1 WorkshopSubmission)

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the ALPFA Convention Portal.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

roleId.description: Reference to the Role assigned to the user. (Relationship: Role 1:N User)

Role.title: Role
Role.description: Represents a user role within the ALPFA Convention Portal (e.g., client, internal, regular user).

permissions.description: A list of permission IDs associated with this role.

Permission.title: Permission
Permission.description: Represents a specific permission within the ALPFA Convention Portal.

description.description: A description of what the permission allows.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification for users or internal teams.

userId.description: Reference to the user who should receive the notification. (Relationship: User 1:N Notification)

timestamp.description: The date and time when the notification was created.

isRead.description: Indicates whether the notification has been read by the user.

FeedbackQueue.title: FeedbackQueue
FeedbackQueue.description: Represents a queue for AI feedback requests related to workshop submissions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the feedback queue.

submissionId.description: Reference to the WorkshopSubmission associated with this queue. (Relationship: WorkshopSubmission 1:1 FeedbackQueue)

question.description: The question submitted by the user to the AI feedback tool.

answer.description: The answer provided by the AI feedback tool.

status.description: The status of the feedback request (e.g., 'pending', 'answered', 'reviewed').

timestamp.description: The date and time when the feedback request was created.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. 'userId' corresponds to the Firebase Auth UID. Includes denormalized 'roleId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores role definitions. Roles are manually assigned and used for authorization.

params.name: roleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the role (e.g., 'admin', 'client', 'user').

definition.description: Stores workshop submissions for each user. Uses path-based ownership for secure access. Includes denormalized `userId` to enforce ownership. Includes denormalized `roleId` from `/users/{userId}` for role based filtering.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who submitted the workshop.

params.name: workshopSubmissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the workshop submission.

definition.description: Stores notifications for each user. Uses path-based ownership. The 'userId' field within the notification document is redundant but maintained for potential future use cases.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who receives the notification.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the notification.

definition.description: Stores AI feedback queue entries.  Accessed by admins and potentially the user who created the submission, based on submissionId.

params.name: feedbackQueueId
params.description: The unique identifier for the feedback queue entry.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability, adhering to the principles of DBAC (Database Authentication and Authorization Control) and QAPs (Rules are not Filters).  Authorization Independence is achieved by denormalizing role information. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, enabling atomic operations and simplifying debugging. Segregation ensures all documents in a collection share the same security posture. User roles (admin, client, end user) are managed through the `roles` collection and linked to users via the `users` collection. Workshop submissions are stored under `/users/{userId}/workshopSubmissions/{workshopSubmissionId}` to represent the 1:N relationship, with roles duplicated to enable independent authorization. This structure supports QAPs by allowing secure list operations based on role and ownership. Notifications are stored under `/users/{userId}/notifications/{notificationId}` to directly tie notifications to users and are designed for individual access. The AI feedback queue is kept separate to manage access and ensure the structure promotes clean access modeling and simplifies future scaling.
